summary: Add multiplayer RPC annotation to a method
prompt: The player script below runs locally. Add multiplayer synchronization: decorate `set_position_on_server` with `@rpc("any_peer", "call_local", "reliable")` so any peer can call it. Add a `sync_position()` method that calls `set_position_on_server.rpc(global_position)` to broadcast to all peers. In `set_position_on_server(pos: Vector2)`, set `global_position = pos`. Also add `is_local_player() -> bool` that returns `multiplayer.get_unique_id() == get_multiplayer_authority()`.
evaluation_notes: Must use @rpc annotation syntax (not rpc() function call). Calling .rpc() on the method is correct Godot 4 syntax. multiplayer.get_unique_id() is correct. get_multiplayer_authority() not get_network_master().

api_check.notes: In Godot 3 the equivalent was get_network_unique_id(). Godot 4 uses multiplayer.get_unique_id(). Also get_network_master was renamed to get_multiplayer_authority.
